_note: Channel manifest for recursive.eco. This makes the repo self-describing: recursive.eco can 'import this repo as a channel' by reading this file from raw.githubusercontent.com/<owner>/<repo>/<branch>/recursive-eco.json. It declares the channel's identity (rendered as a channel-document, document_data.is_channel=true), where its grammars live, and who maintains it. Membership lives in `tools` (channel_slug). Modeled on recursive-tarot/recursive-eco.json and recursive-astrology/recursive-eco.json — the `grammars.paths` glob and `id_map` path are this repo's own (grammars/*/grammar.json, ids.json at root), matching astro's convention rather than tarot's tarot/_eco_ids.json. TODO before this is live: confirm the exact channel-import contract with someone who's actually run a recursive.eco channel import (this manifest is modeled on the sibling sites' shape, not verified against the importer code in this session).

channel.name: The Recursive I Ching
channel.description: A public-domain library of the I Ching — the 64 hexagrams in full, a contemplative reading practice, and a cross-lens synthesis with Human Design gates, zodiac signs, and chakras. Bound to its GitHub repo.

hero.title: The Recursive I Ching
hero.subtitle: Read a hexagram to know yourself, not to be told your fate — the 64 changes, held as a mirror.
hero.body_md:
The classical text in full, a contemplative reading practice built from it, and a cross-lens synthesis against Human Design gates, zodiac signs, and chakras. Built in the open and bound to its GitHub repo — fork it, correct it, or read with it.

A hexagram is a mirror, not a fate: read to reflect, never to obey.

grammars._exclude_note: All four grammars in this repo are real, castable/readable content — none are generated meta or reference-only apparatus, so nothing is excluded (unlike tarot's all-decks-many-lenses / tree-of-tarot / people-of-tarot / books-of-tarot, which are meta grammars this repo has no equivalent of yet).
